source: Adapted from LearnPrompt/ai-news-radar public AI source strategy plus ecommerce-specific AI tool sources.

policy.homepage_default: Only show items that can be mapped to ecommerce operations, content production, ads creative, product research, customer service, store automation, or seller workflow improvement.

sources.name: OpenAI News
sources.why_track: Model, multimodal, agent and automation updates can affect product images, ad creatives, scripts, customer service and ecommerce workflow automation.
sources.risk_note: Generic model news must be filtered out unless it has a clear ecommerce use case.

sources.name: Google DeepMind Blog
sources.why_track: Vision, video, multimodal and reasoning updates may map to ecommerce creative production, product analysis and automation.
sources.risk_note: Research-heavy source; homepage and daily report require ecommerce scenario mapping.

sources.name: Google AI Blog
sources.why_track: Google AI product and capability updates can affect search ads, shopping discovery, multimodal content and merchant workflows.
sources.risk_note: Broad AI feed; generic announcements are filtered out.

sources.name: Hugging Face Blog
sources.why_track: Open models and tooling can lower the cost of ecommerce image, video, text, customer service and automation workflows.
sources.risk_note: Developer-heavy feed; only practical ecommerce capability changes should surface.

sources.name: GitHub AI & ML
sources.why_track: Agent, coding and automation updates may affect ecommerce data collection, store tooling and internal operation workflows.
sources.risk_note: Developer source; avoid generic engineering posts without commerce value.

sources.name: GitHub Changelog
sources.why_track: Copilot, Actions and automation changes can affect agent-assisted ecommerce operations tooling.
sources.risk_note: Very broad changelog; ecommerce scenario mapping is mandatory.

sources.name: NVIDIA Generative AI Blog
sources.why_track: Generative AI deployment updates may affect image/video production cost, speed and local workflow feasibility.
sources.risk_note: Infrastructure-heavy source; only keep items with a practical ecommerce use case.

sources.name: Anthropic News
sources.why_track: Claude model and agent capability changes can map to ecommerce scripts, customer support, analysis and workflow automation.
sources.risk_note: No stable RSS in this project path; keep as candidate until a stable adapter is used.

sources.name: Simon Willison
sources.why_track: Practical AI tooling and agent implementation signals for automation workflows.
sources.risk_note: Default AI radar source. Ecommerce homepage and daily report still require ecommerce-use scoring.

sources.name: Canva Changelog
sources.why_track: Creative and AI design updates can directly affect ecommerce product images, ad creatives and social content production.
sources.risk_note: No verified RSS; use web adapter only if it extracts detail-page links and true publish dates.

sources.name: Runway Blog
sources.why_track: Video generation changes can affect short-video commerce, product demos and ad creative testing.
sources.risk_note: Candidate only until stable feed or detail-page adapter is verified.

sources.name: HeyGen Blog
sources.why_track: Avatar and video generation changes can affect product explainers, live-commerce clips and multilingual ecommerce content.
sources.risk_note: Candidate only until stable feed or detail-page adapter is verified.

sources.name: The Decoder
sources.why_track: AI product, model and agent coverage with practical capability signals.
sources.risk_note: Default AI radar source. Ecommerce homepage and daily report still require ecommerce-use scoring.

sources.name: VentureBeat AI
sources.why_track: AI business and product coverage that can surface AI commerce tooling, ads, automation and customer experience changes.
sources.risk_note: Default AI radar source. Ecommerce homepage and daily report still require ecommerce-use scoring.

sources.name: TechCrunch AI
sources.why_track: AI startup and product coverage; useful for new ecommerce AI tools and platform shifts.
sources.risk_note: Default AI radar source. Ecommerce homepage and daily report still require ecommerce-use scoring.

sources.name: MIT Technology Review AI
sources.why_track: AI trend and capability coverage; keep for broader AI radar and only promote to ecommerce when use case is clear.
sources.risk_note: Default AI radar source. Ecommerce homepage and daily report still require ecommerce-use scoring.

sources.name: AI HOT
sources.why_track: High-signal curated AI feed for the dedicated AI intelligence panel.
sources.risk_note: Default AI radar source. Ecommerce homepage and daily report still require ecommerce-use scoring.

sources.name: MarkTechPost
sources.why_track: Broad AI research and product feed; useful for AI radar, ecommerce promotion requires clear use case.
sources.risk_note: Default AI radar source. Ecommerce homepage and daily report still require ecommerce-use scoring.

sources.name: Meta AI Blog
sources.why_track: Llama, multimodal and social-commerce AI updates can affect ad creative, recommendation and reels-commerce workflows.
sources.risk_note: Default AI radar source. Ecommerce homepage and daily report still require ecommerce-use scoring.

sources.name: AWS Machine Learning Blog
sources.why_track: AWS Bedrock, Rekognition and ecommerce-adjacent AI infrastructure updates that can affect seller workflow automation.
sources.risk_note: Broad ML blog. Only items with clear ecommerce application should surface beyond AI radar.

sources.name: The Batch (DeepLearning.AI)
sources.why_track: Weekly curated AI digest with strong practical signal; useful for AI radar overview.
sources.risk_note: Default AI radar source. Ecommerce homepage and daily report still require ecommerce-use scoring.

sources.name: Import AI (Jack Clark)
sources.why_track: Technical AI newsletter covering capability changes; useful for identifying model-level shifts that could affect ecommerce tooling.
sources.risk_note: Technical and research-heavy. Ecommerce use-case mapping is mandatory before promoting to homepage or daily brief.

sources.name: Towards AI
sources.why_track: Practical AI tutorials and product updates; occasionally surfaces automation and creative production tools relevant to ecommerce.
sources.risk_note: Default AI radar source. Ecommerce homepage and daily report still require ecommerce-use scoring.

sources.name: Zapier Blog AI Automation
sources.why_track: Automation workflow and AI integration updates; directly applicable to ecommerce internal operations, data syncing and agent-based store management.
sources.risk_note: Generally ecommerce-applicable; still filter for specific seller/merchant/store relevance.

sources.name: Shopify Engineering Blog
sources.why_track: Shopify AI, automation and platform capability updates with direct ecommerce merchant impact.
sources.risk_note: Candidate. Verify RSS is accessible and not behind auth before enabling in production run.

sources.name: Microsoft Research Blog
sources.why_track: Azure AI, Copilot and multimodal research updates that may affect enterprise ecommerce tooling.
sources.risk_note: Research-heavy. Only items with a clear ecommerce workflow or creative production use case should surface.

sources.name: AI Business
sources.why_track: Enterprise AI product and deployment coverage; useful for spotting AI commerce tools adopted at scale.
sources.risk_note: Default AI radar source. Ecommerce homepage and daily report still require ecommerce-use scoring.

sources.name: LM Arena / LMSYS Blog
sources.why_track: Model benchmark signals that correlate with capability jumps relevant to ecommerce AI tooling choices.
sources.risk_note: RSS URL unverified. Keep as candidate_only until feed is confirmed stable.
